Class CsvAnimalRepository

java.lang.Object
shelter.repository.csv.CsvAnimalRepository
All Implemented Interfaces:
AnimalRepository
public class CsvAnimalRepository extends Object implements AnimalRepository
CSV-backed implementation of AnimalRepository that persists animal records to a flat file named animals.csv. All concrete animal types (Dog, Cat, Rabbit, and Other) are stored in the same file using a species discriminator column: standard species write their enum name (e.g. DOG), while Other animals write their free-form speciesName so the correct type can be reconstructed on reload. Species-specific columns are left empty for non-applicable types. Records are loaded into memory at construction and flushed on every mutation.

    Constructs a new CsvAnimalRepository that reads from and writes to the given data directory.

  • Constructor Details

    • CsvAnimalRepository

      public CsvAnimalRepository(String dataDir)
      Constructs a new CsvAnimalRepository that reads from and writes to the given data directory. If the CSV file does not yet exist it is created with just the header row; existing data is loaded into memory immediately upon construction.
      Parameters:
      dataDir - the path to the directory that contains (or will contain) the CSV file; must not be null or blank
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dataDir is null or blank
      RuntimeException - if the directory cannot be created or the file cannot be read

  • Method Details

    • save

      public void save(Animal animal)
      Persists a new animal or overwrites the existing record with the same ID. Callers are responsible for ensuring the animal is fully constructed before saving.
      Specified by:
      save in interface AnimalRepository
      Parameters:
      animal - the animal to save; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if animal is null

    • findById

      public Optional<Animal> findById(String id)
      Returns the animal with the given ID, or an empty optional if not found. Does not throw if the ID is absent — callers decide how to handle the missing case.
      Specified by:
      findById in interface AnimalRepository
      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier to look up; must not be null or blank
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the animal, or empty if not found
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if id is null or blank

    • findAll

      public List<Animal> findAll()
      Returns all animals currently persisted in the store. Returns an empty list if no animals have been saved.
      Specified by:
      findAll in interface AnimalRepository
      Returns:
      a list of all animals

    • findByShelterId

      public List<Animal> findByShelterId(String shelterId)
      Returns all animals whose shelter ID matches the given value. Returns an empty list if no animals are associated with that shelter.
      Specified by:
      findByShelterId in interface AnimalRepository
      Parameters:
      shelterId - the shelter ID to filter by; must not be null or blank
      Returns:
      a list of animals in the specified shelter
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if shelterId is null or blank

    • findByAdopterId

      public List<Animal> findByAdopterId(String adopterId)
      Returns all animals that have been adopted by the adopter with the given ID. Returns an empty list if the adopter has not adopted any animals.
      Specified by:
      findByAdopterId in interface AnimalRepository
      Parameters:
      adopterId - the adopter ID to filter by; must not be null or blank
      Returns:
      a list of animals adopted by the specified adopter
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if adopterId is null or blank

    • delete

      public void delete(String id)
      Removes the animal record with the given ID from the store. Does nothing if no animal with that ID exists.
      Specified by:
      delete in interface AnimalRepository
      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the animal to delete; must not be null or blank
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if id is null or blank
